geopolitics_summary: Strait of Hormuz closure and Iran tensions drive LNG price divergence; EU methane rules face exemption pressure; Belgium nuclear support signals capacity concerns.

themes.headline: International LNG prices rise amid Strait of Hormuz closure
themes.why_it_matters: Hormuz closure since Feb 28 widens Europe–Asia LNG arbitrage, raising TTF. EU sourcing costs via LNG spike directly if supply redirects east; DA gas spreads widen as security premium embeds.

themes.headline: Belgium eyes nuclear takeover to keep reactors running
themes.why_it_matters: State takeover suggests aging fleet maintenance capex burden. Reduced private-sector nuclear capex; increased dependence on gas-fired back-up during outages pressures Cal+1 power curve and storage drawdown.

themes.headline: EU floats making it easy for oil companies to break methane rules
themes.why_it_matters: Draft exemptions on 'energy security grounds' weaken supply-side methane discipline. If upstream operators exploit exemptions, fugitive emissions rise but fuel availability may improve; net effect on TTF supply/demand unclear but signals softer enforcement.

themes.headline: Trump bets on quick Iran oil crunch; experts see prolonged pain
themes.why_it_matters: Prolonged Iran sanctions create sustained crude scarcity. EU fuel-switching from gas to crude-based heating oil compresses gas demand but raises coal/power costs; LNG competition for spot cargoes tightens NW Europe winter pricing into Cal+1.

themes.headline: US Strategic Petroleum Reserve releases 17.5M barrels since March
themes.why_it_matters: Large SPR drawdown caps crude rally and reduces incentive for LNG-to-crude switching. Mildly bearish for EU power if crude prices stabilize and coal import arbitrage weakens; modest indirect DA spread compression.

watchlist: Hormuz corridor reopening status; impact on LNG flows to NW Europe and TTF price reset.
Belgium nuclear takeover timeline and implications for winter capacity and gas-fired margin calls.
EU methane exemption finalisation; risk of upstream supply relief offsetting enforcement losses.
Trump–EU auto tariff resolution and spillover into energy trade tensions (ETS credits, renewables equipment).
